evidence.scientific_clarity: Violin+box+jitter overlay clearly shows distribution, median, and spread across all 4 conditions in the correct Control→Drug-A 10µM→Drug-A 50µM→Combo order; significance brackets add statistical context.
evidence.typography: gen_figure.py never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams; PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type3 (bitmapped glyph paths), so text is not selectable/editable; default DejaVu Sans font used throughout.
evidence.palette: Green (#6ACC65) and red (#D65F5F) are used for adjacent conditions, which is problematic for deuteranopia/protanopia (~8% of males); blue and purple pair may also confuse tritanopes; no colorblind-safe palette (e.g. Wong or Okabe-Ito) was applied.
evidence.layout_density: 6×4.5 inch figure with tight_layout; four violins fill the panel well; significance brackets stack cleanly above data; no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes violin+box+jitter+significance brackets and matches the figure; n=100 per group stated; however, y-axis label capitalisation depends on data.json metric field and condition labels use µM (correct); no legend needed as x-axis labels suffice.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains real vector path streams (decompressed stream shows m/l/c/h/f operators and bezier curves); no raster image XObjects; but fonts are Type3 (not TrueType/fonttype=42), so text is not selectable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (28 KB vector), figure.png (63 KB raster), figure.svg (94 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states n=100 per group, describes all visual elements (violin KDE, IQR box, median bold line, jittered points α=0.35), names the statistical test (Mann–Whitney U, two-sided) and significance thresholds; specific and informative.

overall_strengths: Well-constructed violin+box+jitter composite that faithfully implements the brief, with correct condition ordering and significance brackets.
Caption is thorough and publication-ready, naming all visual encodings, sample size, and statistical test with thresholds.
overall_weaknesses: fonttype=42 not set: PDF fonts are Type3 (non-selectable glyphs), and the default DejaVu Sans is used instead of a publication-quality font (e.g. Helvetica, Arial, or a serif).
Palette uses red+green for adjacent conditions, failing colorblind accessibility for the most common forms of color vision deficiency.
